ud1.1:1.2:
on one occasion, the Blessed One was staying at Uruvelā on the
bank of the Nerañjarā River at the root of the Bodhi tree–
the tree of awakening–newly awakened.
ud1.1:1.3:
And on that occasion he sat at the root of the Bodhi tree for seven days in one session,
sensitive to the bliss of release.
ud1.1:1.4:
Then, with the passing of seven days, after emerging from that concentration,
in the first watch of the night, he gave close attention to dependent co-arising in forward order, thus:
ud1.1:2.1:
When this is, that is.
From the arising of this comes the arising of that.
In other words:
ud1.1:2.2: From ignorance as a requisite condition come fabrications.
ud1.1:2.3: From fabrications as a requisite condition comes consciousness.
ud1.1:2.4: From consciousness as a requisite condition comes name-&-form.
ud1.1:2.5: From name-&-form as a requisite condition come the six sense media.
ud1.1:2.6: From the six sense media as a requisite condition comes contact.
ud1.1:2.7: From contact as a requisite condition comes feeling.
ud1.1:2.8: From feeling as a requisite condition comes craving.
ud1.1:2.9: From craving as a requisite condition comes clinging/sustenance.
ud1.1:2.10: From clinging/sustenance as a requisite condition comes becoming.
ud1.1:2.11: From becoming as a requisite condition comes birth.
ud1.1:2.12:
From birth as a requisite condition, then aging-&-death, sorrow,
lamentation, pain, distress, and despair come into play.
ud1.1:2.13: Such is the origination of this entire mass of suffering & stress.
ud1.1:3.1: Then, on realizing the significance of that, the Blessed One on that occasion exclaimed:
ud1.1:4.4: when he discerns a phenomenon with its cause.
